steps.title: Keep the verbs parallel
steps.content: The relative pronoun which refers to plural baskets. In the clause, the baskets typically are woven from sumac and also have bold colors, so the second verb should be present tense and agree with a plural subject.

steps.title: Reject tense shifts
steps.content:
• Choice B shifts to past perfect, which does not match the present-tense description.
  • Choice C uses past progressive, also breaking the tense of the sentence.
  • Choice D uses simple past, while the surrounding clause describes what the baskets typically do or have.
The answer is A.
